title: Structural basis for pH-responsive amino acid transport via SLC7A4.
task: Determine pH-sensing mechanism and amino acid selectivity of SLC7A4 transporter
key_results: Cryo-EM structures of plant homologue CAT4 reveal allosteric mechanism linking ligand recognition to pH regulation; identified residues controlling leucine selectivity in human SLC7A4
comments: Homology modeling of human transporter from plant structure introduces some uncertainty, though supported by transport assays and MD simulations.

inputs: CAT4 (plant homologue) in apo and L-ornithine-bound states

hypotheses.hypothesis: SLC7A4 is pH-regulated leucine transporter functioning via allosteric mechanism
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through structural, computational, and functional evidence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Specific residues mediate amino acid selectivity and pH sensing
hypotheses.result: Identified via structure-guided mutagenesis and MD simulations

title: An information-theoretic argument for the restriction of the current biological alphabets to 4 nucleotides and 20 amino acids
task: Predict optimal alphabet size for evolving biopolymers from information-theoretic constraints
key_results: Theoretical analysis predicts 4 nucleotides and ~20 amino acids as necessary alphabet sizes for effective sequence/conformational space exploration; empirical data agrees with predictions
comments: Elegant theoretical framework but relies on effective space estimates that may oversimplify real biological complexity; early life scenarios with smaller alphabets speculated rather than demonstrated.

model_io.model: Energy landscape theory and molecular information theory
outputs: mapping between sequence and conformational spaces
Predictions for intrinsically disordered protein prevalence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Observed biological alphabet sizes (4 nt, 20 aa) are optimal for biopolymer evolution
hypotheses.result: Supported by information-theoretic constraints matching empirical conformational/sequence space estimates

hypotheses.hypothesis: Primitive genetic systems used smaller amino acid alphabets, yielding more intrinsically disordered proteins
hypotheses.result: Theoretical prediction from early-life genetic code evolution models

title: Colony-stimulating factor-2 triggers NNMT-dependent myofibroblast activation in head and neck cancer
task: Characterize CSF2/FOS/NNMT axis as TGF-β-independent pathway driving myofibroblast activation in HNSCC
key_results: CSF2 upregulation in HNSCC correlates with NNMT overexpression in CAFs; CSF2 enhances myoCAF properties in NNMT-dependent manner, independent of TGF-β; CSF2 induces FOS phosphorylation at Ser32, promoting p-FOS nuclear translocation and NNMT transcription; CSF2 blockade partially overcomes TGF-β inhibitor resistance
comments: The identification of a CSF2-driven myoCAF activation axis parallel to TGF-β signaling offers therapeutic opportunity, though the incomplete rescue by CSF2 blockade suggests additional pathways remain active.

model_io.model: Co-culture of HNSCC organoids with paracancerous fibroblasts
HNSCC xenograft with CSF2 or CSF2RA knockdown

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSF2 upregulation in HNSCC drives NNMT overexpression in CAFs and myoCAF activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CSF2 treatment enhanced myoCAF properties in NNMT-dependent manner

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSF2 induces FOS phosphorylation to drive NNMT transcription independent of TGF-β
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CSF2-induced NNMT overexpression largely unaffected by TGF-β manipulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSF2 blockade partially overcomes TGF-β inhibitor resistance in myoCAF-driven context
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CSF2 blockade provided partial rescue of TGF-β inhibitor resistance

title: CAPRIN1-mediated sequestration of NCOA4 mRNA into stress granules drives sorafenib resistance in hepatocellular carcinoma
task: Elucidate CAPRIN1-mediated NCOA4 mRNA sequestration into stress granules driving sorafenib resistance in HCC
key_results: High stress granule score in HCC correlates with aggressive subtypes, poor survival, and sorafenib resistance; β-catenin activates stress granule gene transcription; CAPRIN1 suppresses ferroptosis by recruiting NCOA4 mRNA into SGs, repressing NCOA4 translation; CAPRIN1 disruption restores NCOA4 and sorafenib sensitivity
comments: The mechanistic integration of stress granules, ferroptosis, and sorafenib resistance is compelling, though the basis for ferroptosis suppression specifically through NCOA4 translation repression could be more thoroughly validated.

model_io.model: HCC cells with CAPRIN1 modulation under sorafenib treatment
HCC patient cohort stratified by SG signature

hypotheses.hypothesis: High stress granule activity promotes sorafenib resistance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; elevated SG score correlates with sorafenib resistance

hypotheses.hypothesis: CAPRIN1 recruits NCOA4 mRNA into SGs, repressing NCOA4 translation and suppressing ferroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CAPRIN1 interacts with NCOA4 mRNA via RGG domain and mediates its SG sequestration

hypotheses.hypothesis: CAPRIN1 disruption restores NCOA4 expression and resensitizes tumors to sorafenib
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; genetic disruption of CAPRIN1 restores NCOA4 expression and sorafenib sensitivity

title: ZNRF1 deficiency disrupts Fas ligand trafficking and immune balance
task: Define ZNRF1-Munc18-2-Syntaxin-3 axis controlling FasL trafficking and immune tolerance
key_results: Myeloid-specific Znrf1 deletion causes age-dependent splenomegaly and elevated T-cell responses; ZNRF1 deficiency impairs terminal trafficking/fusion of FasL to cell surface; ZNRF1 E3 activity strengthens Munc18-2–Syntaxin-3 interaction; Znrf1-deficient macrophages show impaired FasL-dependent killing
comments: The work elegantly connects E3 ligase catalytic activity to SNARE complex stability and membrane trafficking, revealing a subtle mechanistic distinction between assembly and function.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ZNRF1 licenses FasL terminal trafficking and surface exposure via E3 ligase activity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ZNRF1 deficiency impairs surface FasL despite elevated total FasL

hypotheses.hypothesis: ZNRF1 E3 activity stabilizes Munc18-2–Syntaxin-3 interaction required for lysosome-related organelle fusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; catalytically inactive C184A mutant fails to restore surface FasL despite similar complex assembly

hypotheses.hypothesis: Znrf1-deficient macrophages display impaired FasL-dependent killing of T cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; defect not rescued by stronger LPS priming

title: NUP93 facilitates the nuclear import of SOX2 to activate G3BP1 transcription and impairs gemcitabine response in pancreatic cancer
task: Elucidate NUP93-mediated nuclear import of SOX2 and gemcitabine resistance in pancreatic cancer
key_results: NUP93 upregulation correlates with poor PDAC patient survival; NUP93/SOX2/G3BP1 axis promotes DNA damage repair and gemcitabine resistance by stabilizing RAD51 mRNA
comments: The axis demonstrates a clear mechanistic link from nuclear import to chemoresistance, though the dependency on G3BP1 as a stress granule component for RAD51 stabilization is somewhat unexpected and warrants deeper investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NUP93 facilitates SOX2 nuclear import via NLS recognition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NUP93 interacts with SOX2 and promotes its nuclear translocation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear SOX2 activates G3BP1 transcription, which stabilizes RAD51 mRNA and promotes DNA repair
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOX2 binds G3BP1 promoter; G3BP1 stabilizes RAD51 mRNA; this promotes gemcitabine resistance

title: Deltex E3 ubiquitin ligase 2 potentiates STING-mediated type I interferon response by K63-linked ubiquitination
task: Characterize DTX2 as a positive regulator of STING-mediated type I interferon response
key_results: Dtx2−/− mice show increased susceptibility to DNA viral infection; DTX2 promotes K63-linked ubiquitination of STING at K236 and K370, facilitating ER-to-Golgi translocation and downstream signaling
comments: The dual role of DTX2 in both innate immunity and anti-tumor responses is well-supported, but specificity of K63-linkage vs. other ubiquitin chain types could be further explored.

model_io.model: Mouse macrophages and MEFs with Dtx2 deletion
Murine head and neck cancer model

hypotheses.hypothesis: DTX2 positively regulates STING signaling through K63-linked ubiquitination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; DTX2 promotes K63-ubiquitination at STING K236 and K370

hypotheses.hypothesis: K63-ubiquitination facilitates STING translocation from ER to Golgi and activates downstream cascades
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ubiquitination promotes STING relocalization and type I IFN response

title: HOXA9 orchestrates EMT and metastasis in oral cancer via transcriptional activation of vimentin and β-catenin signaling
task: Elucidate HOXA9-mediated EMT and metastasis in oral cancer through VIM and Wnt/β-catenin signaling
key_results: HOXA9 upregulation in advanced lymph node-positive OC; HOXA9 knockdown reduces proliferation, migration, invasion and diminishes lung metastasis; HOXA9 transcriptionally activates VIM and facilitates β-catenin nuclear translocation; hypomethylated HOXA9 promoter correlates with upregulation
comments: The study provides strong evidence for HOXA9 as both an epigenetic target and master regulator of EMT, though the direct mechanistic link between HOXA9-VIM activation and β-catenin nuclear translocation could be better defined.

model_io.model: Nude mouse OC xenograft with HOXA9-knockdown cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: HOXA9 transcriptionally activates VIM to promote EMT
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HOXA9 depletion downregulated VIM and EMT markers

hypotheses.hypothesis: HOXA9 facilitates β-catenin nuclear translocation and Wnt/β-catenin cascade activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HOXA9 depletion reduced β-catenin nuclear localization and downstream target activation

hypotheses.hypothesis: HOXA9 promoter hypomethylation in advanced tumors drives upregulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; inverse correlation between hypomethylation and HOXA9 upregulation in stage III/IV tumors; activating histone marks enriched at promoter

title: Everolimus suppresses glucose transporter 3 membrane trafficking to improve therapeutic efficacy of umbilical cord blood-derived mesenchymal stem cell transplantation in diabetic retinopathy
task: Enhance UCB-MSC survival and therapeutic efficacy in diabetic retinopathy via mTOR inhibition
key_results: Everolimus reduced glucose uptake and mtROS production in high glucose conditions; improved retinal function in STZ-induced DR rat model with GLUT3 knockdown or everolimus-pretreated MSCs
comments: The mechanistic link between cofilin phosphorylation and GLUT3 membrane trafficking is interesting, though the clinical translation from rat models remains to be demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Everolimus enhances MSC viability by inhibiting GLUT3-mediated glucose uptake and mtROS production
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; everolimus suppressed GLUT3 membrane translocation and reduced mtROS and cell death

hypotheses.hypothesis: mTORC1 inhibition reduces actin stabilization and GLUT3 translocation via cofilin phosphorylation downregulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; everolimus inhibited mTORC1, reduced cofilin phosphorylation, and suppressed GLUT3 translocation

title: LMO7-mediated POLR2A degradation promotes cellular senescence through the MDM4/p53/p21 axis
task: Determine LMO7-mediated POLR2A degradation drives cellular senescence via MDM4/p53/p21 axis
key_results: POLR2A declines in senescent cells and aging tissues; POLR2A depletion induces senescence in p53-dependent manner; LMO7 E3 ligase recruits POLR2A for K-ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation; MDM4 mediates p53 upregulation
comments: The identification of LMO7 as a POLR2A-targeting E3 ligase is novel, though the question of how POLR2A loss specifically triggers MDM4 upregulation (vs. general transcription decline) deserves deeper investigation.

model_io.model: H₂O₂-induced senescent cells with LMO7 depletion

hypotheses.hypothesis: POLR2A depletion induces p53-dependent cellular senescence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; p53 co-depletion rescues senescence phenotype

hypotheses.hypothesis: MDM4 mediates p53 upregulation upon POLR2A knockdown
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; bioinformatic and functional studies identified MDM4 as key mediator

hypotheses.hypothesis: LMO7 E3 ligase promotes POLR2A ubiquitination and degradation in senescent cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; LMO7 depletion abolishes POLR2A ubiquitination in H₂O₂-induced senescence

title: A bridge-like lipid transfer protein is critical for generation of invasive stages in malaria parasites
task: Characterize PfVPS13L1 lipid transfer protein in generating malaria invasive stages via IMC formation
key_results: PfVPS13L1 is a VAP-interacting bridge-like lipid transfer protein at ER-MCSs; PfVPS13L1 mediates bulk lipid transfer from ER to nascent inner membrane complex (IMC); loss-of-function reduces IMC growth and produces smaller anucleated progeny, impairing schizogony
comments: The work identifies a critical lipid trafficking mechanism in apicomplexan parasites, though the specific lipid cargo transferred and the regulation of VPS13L1 activity remain to be elucidated.

model_io.model: P. falciparum blood stage parasites with PfVPS13L1 manipulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: PfVPS13L1 mediates bulk lipid transfer at ER-MCSs with the nascent IMC
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PfVPS13L1 bridges ER and IMC for lipid distribution

hypotheses.hypothesis: PfVPS13L1-mediated lipid transfer is critical for IMC growth and schizogony
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PfVPS13L1 loss reduces IMC growth and impairs generation of invasive progeny

title: 7SK-enriched exosomes suppress the cancer phenotypes in human lung cancer cells: insights from 2D and microfluidic 3D in vitro models
task: Demonstrate exosome-mediated 7SK delivery suppresses lung cancer phenotypes
key_results: Exo-7SK elevated 7SK levels and reduced NSCLC cell viability and proliferation; promoted apoptosis and inhibited migration/invasion; impaired spheroid formation in 3D microfluidic culture
comments: The study effectively uses both 2D and 3D culture models to demonstrate efficacy, but lacks mechanistic insight into how 7SK exerts its anti-cancer effects.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosome-mediated 7SK delivery elevates intracellular 7SK and suppresses cancer phenotypes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Exo-7SK increased 7SK levels and suppressed viability, proliferation, migration, invasion; promoted apoptosis

hypotheses.hypothesis: 7SK suppresses EMT-related gene expression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Exo-7SK shifted gene expression away from EMT

title: Biological effects of 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF exposure on neuronal and glial cell models under isothermal conditions
task: Assess biological effects of 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF on neuronal and glial cells under isothermal conditions
key_results: No statistically significant differences in mitochondrial ROS, cell viability, apoptosis, or astrocyte proliferation at SAR 0.08 and 4 W/kg for 1 or 24 h exposure in astrocytes and neuroblastoma cells; positive controls validated assay sensitivity
comments: The isothermal exposure design is rigorous and controls for thermal confounds, but the relatively short exposure durations and in vitro models may not capture chronic or in vivo effects.

model_io.model: Primary rat cortical astrocytes and human SH-SY5Y neuroblastoma cells exposed to 700 MHz 5G-modulated RF-EMF

hypotheses.hypothesis: 700 MHz 5G-modulated RF-EMF induces oxidative stress, apoptosis, or proliferative changes under isothermal conditions
hypotheses.result: Refuted; no significant biological effects detected at SAR up to 4 W/kg

title: GABARAPL1 is important for the activation of HRI during eIF2α phosphorylation-dependent stress response to sodium arsenite
task: Characterize GABARAPL1's role in HRI-mediated eIF2α phosphorylation during arsenite stress
key_results: GABARAPL1 absence decreased eIF2α phosphorylation and stress granule formation upon sodium arsenite exposure; GABARAPL1 facilitates HSP90-HRI interaction required for full HRI kinase activation
comments: The proposed model elegantly connects an autophagy protein to stress granule formation, though the structural basis for GABARAPL1-mediated HSP90-HRI facilitation requires further clarification.

model_io.model: A549 cells with GABARAPL1 depletion under sodium arsenite stress

hypotheses.hypothesis: GABARAPL1 is required for full HRI kinase activation during arsenite stress
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; GABARAPL1 absence reduced eIF2α phosphorylation

hypotheses.hypothesis: GABARAPL1 facilitates HSP90-HRI interaction necessary for HRI activation
hypotheses.result: Proposed based on observations; direct demonstration of interaction facilitation not yet shown

title: Study on the mechanism of action of HuaZhuoKeLi in modulating LCN2-mediated cellular pyroptosis to ameliorate ulcerative colitis
task: Investigate HuaZhuoKeLi mechanism in ameliorating ulcerative colitis via LCN2-mediated pyroptosis suppression
key_results: HZKL reduced colonic inflammation and improved intestinal barrier integrity in DSS-induced UC; downregulated LCN2 and pyroptosis-related genes; LCN2, ZBP1, CD55, S100A8 identified as UC-associated pyroptosis genes with diagnostic potential; HZKL enhanced epithelial cell proliferation and migration
comments: The bioinformatics-guided identification of pyroptosis-related hub genes is methodologically sound, but the functional specificity of HZKL's multi-component herbal formula toward LCN2 signaling requires component-level validation.

model_io.model: DSS-induced UC mouse model treated with HZKL
LPS-stimulated Caco-2 cells with LCN2 modulation treated with HZKL drug-containing serum

hypotheses.hypothesis: HZKL ameliorates UC by suppressing LCN2-mediated pyroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HZKL treatment downregulated LCN2 and pyroptosis markers; LCN2 identified as key drug-responsive regulator

hypotheses.hypothesis: LCN2 is a hub gene in UC-associated pyroptosis pathways with diagnostic utility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; LCN2 among identified hub genes showing diagnostic potential via ROC analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: HZKL promotes epithelial barrier repair by enhancing cell proliferation and migration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HZKL enhanced Caco-2 proliferation and migration in vitro

title: Small-molecule binding and sensing with a designed protein family
task: Design small-molecule-binding proteins and biosensors using deep learning and physics-based methods
key_results: Designed binders achieving nanomolar to low micromolar binding affinities with atomic-level design accuracy; successfully constructed cortisol biosensor via chemically induced dimerization
comments: Combining deep learning with physics-based design demonstrates practical utility, though scaling to diverse therapeutic targets requires further validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NTF2 fold enables diverse, designable small-molecule binding pockets
hypotheses.result: Six targets successfully designed with nanomolar-low micromolar affinities

hypotheses.hypothesis: CID approach enables biosensor development for small molecules
hypotheses.result: Cortisol detection system successfully constructed

title: Neoantigen evolution and response to checkpoint inhibitor immunotherapy in colorectal cancer
task: Model neoantigen evolution and predict immunotherapy response in colorectal cancer via computational integration
key_results: Strong clonal neoantigen identified as crucial for immunotherapy success; minimal neoantigen quality among all neoantigens is strong predictor of durable response; heterogeneous tumor mutational landscape correlates with therapeutic outcomes
comments: Integrating stochastic branching-process models with tumor-immune dynamics is elegant, but clinical validation on independent CCBS cohorts and prospective trials would strengthen the practical utility for predicting responders.

model_io.model: Stochastic branching-process model of neoantigen evolution
outputs: in-silico tumor neoantigen landscapes with immunogenicity labels

model_io.model: Dynamical systems model of tumor-immune interactions under checkpoint-blockade

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strong clonal neoantigen is crucial for successful checkpoint-blockade response
hypotheses.result: Clonal neoantigen presence strongly associated with durable response

hypotheses.hypothesis: Minimal neoantigen quality across all neoantigens predicts durable response
hypotheses.result: Minimum neoantigen quality identified as one of strongest response predictors

title: A cough sound-based deep learning algorithm for accessible prompt detection of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with smartphones
task: Detect COPD from voluntary cough sounds using transformer-based deep learning
key_results: Internal validation AUC 0.92, external validation AUC 0.94; 92% sensitivity, 86% specificity on external cohort; sensitivity >91% across GOLD stages; robust across non-COPD respiratory conditions and phone models
comments: Excellent diagnostic performance and robustness across conditions demonstrates genuine clinical potential, though generalization to diverse global populations and spirometry availability remains a consideration.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cough sound analysis via deep learning enables sensitive COPD detection
hypotheses.result: 92% sensitivity and 86% specificity on external validation (105 COPD, 617 non-COPD)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Algorithm generalizes across COPD stages and non-COPD respiratory conditions
hypotheses.result: Sensitivity >91% for GOLD 1-4; maintained accuracy across respiratory conditions and phone models

title: The SjD Map: an interactive pathway tour into Sjögren’s disease signalling mechanisms
task: Construct comprehensive Molecular Interaction Map for Sjögren's disease signalling mechanisms
key_results: 1,625 DEGs identified across three datasets; 25 shared genes across all datasets; 9 genes linked to interferon signaling; 137 pathways analyzed; MIM contains 829 entities and 598 interactions
comments: Multi-dataset integration strengthens confidence in core findings, and the interactive online resource is valuable, but causal relationships and therapeutic target validation require functional studies.

model_io.model: Differential expression analysis (3 cohorts: GSE51092, UKPSSR, PRECISESADS)
inputs: peripheral blood RNA-seq from SjD patients and controls

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interferon signaling is a pivotal pathway in SjD pathogenesis
hypotheses.result: Nine common DEGs linked to interferon signaling across all datasets

title: KinForm: kinetics-informed feature optimised representation models for enzyme kcat and KM prediction
task: Improve enzyme kinetic parameter prediction (kcat, KM) via optimized protein feature representations
key_results: KinForm outperformed baseline methods; improvements most pronounced in low sequence similarity bins; best performance on benchmark datasets with fold sequence overlap removal
comments: Multi-embedding approach with intermediate-layer selection and binding-site-weighted pooling is methodologically sound, though benchmark datasets remain limited in scale and the clinical impact for enzyme engineering is unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Binding-site-weighted pooling and intermediate-layer selection improve kinetic predictions
hypotheses.result: Improvements observed; most pronounced in low sequence similarity scenarios

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sequence overlap between folds inflates generalization estimates
hypotheses.result: Fold-based splitting provides more realistic benchmarking than random splitting

title: From short-lived to persistent: the significance of extracellular RNA in disinfected drinking water microbiomes
task: Characterize adaptive responses and antibiotic resistance in disinfected drinking water microbiomes via extracellular RNA
key_results: Energy efficiency, membrane composition dynamics, oxidative stress response, and protein folding identified as key survival features; antibiotic resistance genes expressed and upregulated in intracellular RNA; regulatory functions observed in non-coding and transfer-messenger RNA
comments: Using eRNA as a reference baseline to infer adaptive features is innovative, but mechanistic validation of identified pathways and their contribution to survival under disinfectant stress requires targeted experiments.

inputs: extracellular RNA and intracellular RNA from drinking water microbiomes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extracellular RNA enables inference of important adaptive features in disinfected water systems
hypotheses.result: Energy efficiency, membrane dynamics, oxidative stress response, and protein folding identified as critical

hypotheses.hypothesis: Selection pressure favors antibiotic resistance gene expression in drinking water microbiomes
hypotheses.result: Antibiotic resistance genes expressed and upregulated in intracellular RNA fraction

title: An optimized EfficientNetB0 framework with CLAHE-based preprocessing for accurate multi-class chest X-ray classification
task: Multi-label chest X-ray classification for concurrent pathologies
key_results: Macro-average AUC of 0.906, recall of 0.824; outperformed DenseNet121 and MobileNetV2; Pneumonia AUC=0.950, Cardiomegaly AUC=0.946
comments: The CLAHE-based preprocessing and class balancing approach is sound, but evaluation on NIH dataset alone limits generalizability claims without external validation.

inputs: chest X-ray images with CLAHE preprocessing

hypotheses.hypothesis: CLAHE preprocessing and class balancing improve multi-label classification accuracy
hypotheses.result: Superior performance vs. DenseNet121 and MobileNetV2 demonstrated

title: Machine learning-predicted chromatin organization landscape across pediatric tumors
task: Predict 3D genome folding disruptions caused by somatic structural variants in pediatric tumors
key_results: Identified five recurrently disrupted regions across multiple tumor types; lymphomas and sarcomas showed most disruptive SVs; some highly disrupted regions had low mutation rates but harbored tumor-associated genes
comments: Leveraging Akita CNN for genome-wide SV impact prediction is innovative, though computational predictions require experimental validation for clinical translation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SVs disrupt 3D genome organization differently across tumor types
hypotheses.result: Significant variability observed; lymphomas/sarcomas most disruptive

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-impact SVs cluster in recurrent genomic regions
hypotheses.result: Five recurrently disrupted regions identified despite varying mutation rates

title: The impact of PD-1 mutations on pembrolizumab binding: insights from molecular dynamics and MM-GBSA analysis
task: Characterize impact of PD-1 interface mutations on pembrolizumab binding affinity
key_results: Identified 16 key PD-1 residues; 28 UniProt variants analyzed; 23 mutations reduced binding affinity; Pro89Arg, Asp85Gly, and Asp85Asn showed largest reductions (26.69, 20.33, 18.38 kcal/mol)
comments: MM-GBSA analysis provides useful binding predictions, but lack of experimental validation for the 28 variants limits clinical significance assertions without structural biology studies.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Specific PD-1 mutations significantly alter pembrolizumab binding
hypotheses.result: 23 of 28 mutations reduced binding affinity; Pro89Arg, Asp85Gly, Asp85Asn most disruptive

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pro89Arg disrupts hydrophobic interactions; Asp85 mutations abolish salt bridge
hypotheses.result: MD simulations and MM-GBSA analysis confirmed mechanism of binding disruption

title: Few-shot android malware classification with quantum-enhanced prototypical learning and drift detection
task: Few-shot android malware classification with adaptive concept drift detection and feature selection
key_results: 99.70% accuracy on CCCS-CIC-AndMal-2020 (15 families), 99.33% on KronoDroid (binary); dimensionality reduction 99.46% and 94.07%; temporal stability with max 0.24% accuracy degradation
comments: Comprehensive approach combining few-shot learning, feature selection, and concept drift detection is well-designed, though evaluation on two datasets and lack of comparison with recent quantum-enhanced baselines limits scope.

model_io.model: Prototypical network with episodic meta-learning + quantum-enhanced classification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Few-shot prototypical learning with feature selection enables high-accuracy malware detection
hypotheses.result: 99.70% accuracy on CCCS-CIC-AndMal-2020; outperformed existing methods by 0.70–9.70%

hypotheses.hypothesis: Concept drift detection maintains temporal stability
hypotheses.result: Maximum accuracy degradation of 0.24% across evaluation periods

title: Metabolite correlation-based network analysis combined with machine learning techniques highlights LOX biosynthesis in Vanilla planifolia and Vanilla pompona source leaves
task: Identify metabolic pathway differences and LOX biosynthesis variation between vanilla species
key_results: 544 metabolites profiled; higher network connectivity in V. pompona (6425 vs. 3508 edges); linoleic acid enriched in V. planifolia; LOX pathway highlighted via CNA+machine learning
comments: Multi-location sampling strengthens findings, but mechanistic validation of species-specific LOX pathway differences and pollinator attraction effects requires functional studies.

inputs: source leaf tissue from V. planifolia and V. pompona

model_io.model: Correlation-based network analysis (CNA) + machine learning pathway mapping

hypotheses.hypothesis: Two vanilla species have distinct central metabolome profiles
hypotheses.result: Clear separation between species confirmed by multivariate and location-adjusted models

hypotheses.hypothesis: V. pompona exhibits modified fatty acid metabolism affecting pollinator attraction
hypotheses.result: Higher network connectivity, linoleic acid variation, and LOX pathway upregulation detected

title: Characterisation of thigh-based electrocardiography (ECG) across different pathologies
task: Assess feasibility of thigh-based ECG monitoring via toilet-seat interface for cardiovascular disease stratification
key_results: 30 hospitalized patients; clear P-QRS-T complexes preserved; HRV metrics reliably extracted; aortic stenosis most distant from normal; valve disease patients showed disease-specific HRV patterns
comments: Novel unobtrusive sensor approach is promising, but sample size is modest and clinical utility for home-based screening requires longitudinal validation in ambulatory populations.

inputs: ECG signals from toilet seat interface

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thigh-based ECG enables reliable signal acquisition and disease differentiation
hypotheses.result: Clear P-QRS-T complexes, HRV extraction, and disease-specific clustering observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Valve diseases exhibit distinct HRV patterns reflecting autonomic differences
hypotheses.result: Tricuspid/mitral involvement showed higher variability; aortic valve disease showed reduced parasympathetic indices

title: Improving road safety in smart cities using machine learning techniques
task: Road safety prediction in smart cities using machine learning on traffic accident data
key_results: XGBoost and Random Forest achieve 74% accuracy on KSI dataset; Random Forest reaches 99% after Grid Search hyperparameter tuning and undersampling; Association Rule Mining identifies risk factors (speeding, aggressive driving, inattentiveness, absent traffic controls)
comments: Practical ML application combining multiple techniques (RF, XGBoost, Association Rules); hyperparameter tuning details crucial but 99% accuracy likely inflated by undersampling bias; Cross-country comparison (Toronto vs. Rawalpindi) adds value.

inputs: traffic accident data (Toronto KSI dataset, Pakistan RTA dataset)
accident features (speed, driving behavior, collision type, controls)
outputs: extracted rules for fatal/major injury associations

hypotheses.hypothesis: XGBoost and Random Forest identify severe injury patterns without hyperparameter tuning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 74% baseline accuracy on KSI dataset

hypotheses.hypothesis: Grid Search and undersampling substantially improve performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Random Forest reaches 99% (though likely biased)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Speeding, aggressive driving, and absent controls are primary risk factors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via Association Rule Mining

title: Optimized environmental prediction in smart buildings using Dynamic Greylag Goose algorithm and deep learning
task: Environmental prediction in smart buildings using Dynamic Greylag Goose optimization and LSTM
key_results: DGGO-LSTM achieves MSE 0.00119 (17–37% lower than baselines: GWO, GGO, WOA), NSE 0.98247; predicts temperature, humidity, air quality, sound, light; 42% faster than WOA-LSTM (145 s vs. 251 s)
comments: Dual optimization (feature selection + hyperparameter tuning via DGGO) is sound; LSTM is standard but DGGO-based tuning is novel application; IoT sensor data preprocessing not detailed.

model_io.model: Dynamic Greylag Goose Optimization (DGGO) binary selector
inputs: multi-sensor features (temperature, humidity, air quality, sound, light)
selected sensor features from public smart building IoT dataset
outputs: environmental predictions (temperature, humidity, AQ, sound, light)

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGGO for binary feature selection reduces input dimensionality without sacrificing predictive signal
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; DGGO-LSTM outperforms baselines

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGGO hyperparameter tuning yields better LSTM configuration than fixed baselines
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 17–37% MSE reduction vs. GWO/GGO/WOA

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGGO is computationally more efficient than other metaheuristics for this task
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 42% speedup vs. WOA-LSTM

title: Numerical study on the performance of a forced ventilation system under hydrogen leakage in an underground hydrogen equipment room
task: Ventilation system design for hydrogen leakage in underground equipment rooms
key_results: RANS simulation of hydrogen diffusion; simple predictive model for volume-averaged H₂ concentration achieves good agreement; guidance on effective ventilation methods for various scenarios
comments: 3D transient CFD is appropriate tool; simple model validation against numerics is sound; practical design recommendations for industrial application.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Simple predictive model captures temporal trends of H₂ concentration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; model predicts times to reach 1 and 4 vol% thresholds

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fan number and inflow vent state significantly affect ventilation performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; parametric study identifies effective configurations

title: Enhancing MTC device security in LTE networks for the fast and secure BC-based group handover authentication protocol
task: Blockchain-based group handover authentication for MTC devices in LTE with relay node optimization
key_results: Chimp Optimization Algorithm (ChOA) selects optimal relay for minimal energy/delay/distance; blockchain ensures authentication integrity/confidentiality; significant reductions in energy consumption, delay, and distance during group handovers
comments: Applies BC and metaheuristic optimization to 5G/LTE security; quantitative performance metrics not detailed; ChOA choice lacks justification vs. other metaheuristics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ChOA optimization reduces energy consumption and delay in relay selection
hypotheses.result: Claimed; detailed comparative metrics not provided

hypotheses.hypothesis: Blockchain decentralization improves authentication security and integrity
hypotheses.result: Supported by protocol design; empirical validation limited

title: Remaining useful life prediction method based on gated dilation causal convolution
task: Remaining useful life prediction using gated dilated causal convolution
key_results: Novel gated dilation causal convolution framework with multi-feature squeeze excitation and sinusoidal position coding; reduces vanishing gradient, improves global/local feature modeling
comments: Addresses real limitation of CNN-based RUL prediction; architectural choices (sinusoidal encoding, gating) are standard but combination is novel; ablation study validates components.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dilated causal convolution reduces vanishing gradient and improves global feature modeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; framework outperforms traditional CNNs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-feature squeeze excitation adaptively recalibrates local and global responses
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via ablation study

title: An embedded deep learning framework for real-time violence detection and alert generation
task: Real-time violence detection on edge devices using CNN-GRU with model compression
key_results: Lightweight CNN + GRU spatiotemporal model; average accuracy 96.9%, F1-score 96.3%, ROC–AUC 0.972 across 5 benchmarks + custom 6-class dataset; 26 FPS, 38.4 ms/frame latency on live video; quantization/pruning/TensorRT optimization maintain accuracy without compromise
comments: Practical embedded implementation with rigorous system-level design; ablation and statistical testing (p<0.05) validate component contributions; Grad-CAM interpretability is strength; CNN-GRU is established architecture but optimization pipeline is novel.

inputs: video frames (Hockey Fight, RLVS, Violent Flows, ShanghaiTech, UCF-Crime, custom 6-class)

hypotheses.hypothesis: CNN-GRU architecture effectively captures spatiotemporal violent action signatures
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 96.9% accuracy across diverse datasets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantization/pruning preserve accuracy while enabling real-time edge deployment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 26 FPS, 38.4 ms latency achieved without accuracy loss

hypotheses.hypothesis: Grad-CAM interpretability localizes violent regions accurately
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via qualitative analysis

title: Mathematical modeling of adaptive information security strategies using composite behavior models
task: Adaptive information security using composite behavior models and game theory
key_results: Unified dynamic model combining attacker and defender behavior; shows improvements in threat detection, mitigation speed, and resource optimization; superior scalability vs. existing methods
comments: Claims of 'strong improvements' are not quantified; simulation-based validation without benchmark comparison weakens empirical support.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified composite model better captures aggregate behavior than isolated models
hypotheses.result: Supported by simulation; quantitative benchmarks not provided

hypotheses.hypothesis: Game theory-derived adaptation rules enable proactive defense
hypotheses.result: Claimed; lacks rigorous validation against established baselines

title: Exploring the importance of shape on dynamic recognition of self-face or friend-face
task: Investigate role of face shape vs. motion in recognizing self vs. friend faces
key_results: Self-face motion identification strongly depends on face shape; friend-face motion recognition independent of observed shape; self-faces have distinct perceptual characteristics
comments: Well-designed experiment using Deepfakes to isolate shape vs. motion; finds clear dissociation between self and friend-face processing, with implications for face perception theory.

model_io.model: Psychophysical experiment with Deepfake-manipulated face videos
inputs: dynamic face clips with shape/motion manipulated independently

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-face motion identification depends more on shape than friend-face recognition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; self-face requires shape match for clear identification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Friend-face motion recognition is shape-independent
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; motion recognition unaffected by observed shape

title: RGB-conditioned frequency domain refinement for sparse-to-dense depth completion
task: Depth completion from sparse measurements using frequency-domain refinement
key_results: State-of-the-art performance with sharp edges and suppressed texture artifacts; decouples structure from texture via wavelet decomposition and content-adaptive filtering
comments: Novel frequency-domain approach elegantly avoids texture copying; wavelet-based conditioning is well-motivated but computational overhead not discussed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoupling structure (low-frequency) from texture (high-frequency) reduces texture artifacts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; method achieves state-of-the-art with sharp edges

hypotheses.hypothesis: RGB as conditioning signal rather than direct feature fusion improves quality
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; suppresses texture copying artifacts

title: Confidence-calibrated federated graph attention for internet of things agents under latency SLOs
task: Federated graph attention for IoT agents under latency SLOs with trust-weighted aggregation
key_results: ROC-AUC/PR-AUC ≈0.97–0.98 with improved calibration (low ECE); p99 latency 27.5 ms (enforcement) vs. 39.2 ms baseline; parameter-efficient ≤1.1 MB/round updates; SLO compliance <100 ms verified on edge hardware (Raspberry Pi 5, Jetson Orin Nano, Intel NUC 11)
comments: Addresses practical deployment challenge of latency-predictable federated learning; detailed latency breakdown and energy accounting (crypto overhead quantified) strengthen credibility; hardware validation on diverse edge devices is commendable.

inputs: IoMT sensor data from N=100 simulated clients

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trust-weighted secure aggregation maintains calibration under federated setting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves low ECE and ROC-AUC 0.97–0.98

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parameter-efficient LoRA updates satisfy <100 ms SLO on edge devices
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; p99=27.5 ms enforcement latency measured on real hardware

hypotheses.hypothesis: CKKS+SMPC encryption overhead quantifiable and acceptable for use case
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Δp99≈26 ms, +5.8 J/round on Raspberry Pi 5

title: IoT enabled offshore infrastructure management and its impact on monitoring maintenance safety compliance and structural resilience
task: Evaluating IoT implementation impact on offshore infrastructure management
key_results: IoT significantly improves monitoring visibility, supports data-driven maintenance, enhances safety compliance; resilience gains linked to early fault detection rather than direct structural diagnostics
comments: Questionnaire-based study lacks quantitative metrics; causal inference from survey data is limited and findings are largely qualitative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IoT implementation improves operational monitoring capability and safety compliance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; survey results indicate significant improvements

hypotheses.hypothesis: Resilience improvements come primarily from predictive maintenance, not structural changes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; early fault detection is key mechanism

title: Evaluating multimodal commercial and open-source large language models for dynamical astronomy: a benchmark study of resonant behavior classification
task: Multimodal LLM benchmark for resonant behavior classification in orbital dynamics
key_results: Commercial LLMs reach F₁=100% on clear cases, 94% on three-class ambiguous dataset; best open-source F₁≈90–96% on binary, 76% on complex; no training/fine-tuning required; small locally runnable models achieve 'practically useful' accuracy
comments: Systematic evaluation is rigorous; benchmark datasets (RB-TEST, RB-PILOT, RB-SMALL, RB-FULL) are reusable contribution; claim that LLMs match classical ML is strong but transient/resonance-sticking regimes remain error-prone.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Commercial LLMs outperform open-source on ambiguous/transient cases due to scale
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; F₁ gap 94% vs. 76% on RB-SMALL

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs achieve comparable accuracy to classical ML without explicit training
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; F₁≈90–96% on binary matches classical methods

hypotheses.hypothesis: Most errors occur in transient and resonance-sticking regimes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; identified as failure mode across all tested LLMs

title: Rigorous construction and classification of solitary-waves and exact soliton configurations in the nonlinear coupled Maccari system
task: Construction and classification of solitary-wave and soliton solutions in nonlinear coupled Maccari system
key_results: Generalized exponential rational function (GERF) method derives traveling-wave and soliton solutions; yields non-topological/topological solitons, exponential, kink-type, and periodic singular wave structures
comments: Analytical method appropriate for Schrödinger-related systems; GERF-based reduction from PDEs to ODEs is standard technique; contributes to theoretical understanding of nonlinear wave phenomena but lacks numerical validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GERF method via wave-variable transformation reduces Maccabi PDEs to solvable ODEs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; multiple exact solution classes derived

hypotheses.hypothesis: Solutions include diverse dynamical structures (topological/non-topological solitons, kinks, periodic waves)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; comprehensive solution taxonomy presented

title: A three-level discretization framework for dynamic behaviors of graphene-coated rotational blades with preset-angle sandwich structure under complex loads
task: Dynamic analysis of graphene-coated rotational turbine blades under pulse loads
key_results: Three-level Chebyshev-Ritz-Galerkin discretization transforms PDEs to ODEs; integrates centrifugal effects, material anisotropy, geometric nonlinearity; parametric study reveals synergistic effects of GPL weight fraction and rotational speed
comments: Sophisticated mechanical modeling with graphene reinforcement; strong emphasis on computational efficiency via Chebyshev-Ritz method; practical guidance for turbine blade design under extreme conditions.

inputs: graphene coating parameters (weight fraction, GPL aspect ratio)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chebyshev-Ritz-Galerkin discretization overcomes FEM convergence issues under high-speed rotation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; method ensures high computational efficiency and accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graphene weight fraction and rotational speed have synergistic effects on dynamic response
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via systematic parametric study

title: Topology optimization of wheel spoke cavities for lightweight design under bending fatigue and impact load cases
task: Multi-load topology optimization of wheel spoke cavities for lightweight design
key_results: Region-constrained topology optimization reduces peak von Mises stress: 19.25% for bending fatigue (133.9→108.13 MPa), 14.57% for 13° impact (74.63→63.76 MPa); mass reduction 0.53%; manufacturing-compatible (10 mm min member size, casting draw constraints)
comments: Rigorous compromise programming approach with scientifically calibrated weighting; practical manufacturing constraints integrated; modest mass reduction suggests trade-off between performance and weight.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Compromise programming balances multi-load objectives better than single-objective optimization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves stress reduction for both fatigue and impact

hypotheses.hypothesis: Manufacturing constraints do not compromise structural performance gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; optimization remains effective with casting compatibility

title: A structure-preserving diffusion-based zero-shot learning framework for multimodal magnetic flux leakage signal analysis
task: Pipeline defect detection via zero-shot learning with multimodal MFL fusion
key_results: Structure-Preserving Diffusion Model (SPDM) improves SNR from 12.3 dB to 24.1 dB; macro F1 0.93 on known defects; Zero-Shot Accuracy 0.84, H-Mean 0.88 on unseen defects; cross-material/pipeline ZSL Accuracy 0.81
comments: Sophisticated multimodal fusion (MFL+UT+IR) with semantic attribute space for zero-shot learning is innovative; strong generalization demonstrated but engineering applicability claims need real deployment validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SPDM with triple constraints (gradient, morphology, frequency) preserves defect structure during denoising
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SNR improved 12.3→24.1 dB

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-modal attention mitigates inter-modal redundancy better than early/late fusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; macro F1 0.93 outperforms alternatives

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic attribute space enables knowledge transfer to unseen defect classes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ZSL Accuracy 0.84 with H-Mean 0.88

title: Predictive patterning via solid-state dewetting of transferred single-crystal films
task: Predictively guide solid-state dewetting of single-crystal films for pattern formation
key_results: Anisotropic Pd(100) dewetting yields <001> and <011> facets; <011> favored under oxygen or high H₂ flow; submicron electrode channels demonstrated for thin-film transistors
comments: Multiscale modeling plus crystallography-controlled anisotropy is powerful for device fabrication, though generalization to other materials requires further work.

model_io.model: Multiscale dewetting simulation of single-crystal Pd(100) films

hypotheses.hypothesis: <011> facets dominate under oxidizing or high H₂ conditions due to adsorbate-induced energy landscape shift
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via computation and experiment

title: The role of the Helmholtz potential on electrocatalytic activity
task: Explain electrocatalytic activity via Helmholtz potential and interface thermodynamics
key_results: Physical limit of exchange current density for HER is 10 A cm⁻²; thin semiconductor overlayers (1–10 nm) enhance activity through Helmholtz potential modulation
comments: Bridges classical Butler-Volmer kinetics with interfacial entropy concepts, though experimental validation beyond HER literature data would strengthen claims.

model_io.model: Modified Butler-Volmer formalism with Helmholtz potential

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electrocatalytic activity is governed by both Sabatier principle and Helmholtz potential
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via literature HER experimental data

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thin semiconductor layers enhance activity by modulating Helmholtz potential
hypotheses.result: Supported; explains observed activity improvements

title: Prediction of ambient-pressure high-temperature superconductivity in electronically modified transition-metal hydrides
task: Predict ambient-pressure high-Tc superconductivity in electronically modified transition-metal hydrides
key_results: MgAlFeH₆ predicted Tc ≈ 130 K; average projected DOS (geometric mean of total and H-projected DOS) correlates more strongly with Tc than individual DOS measures
comments: Expands superconductor search space to non-metallic compounds via carrier doping; high-Tc vs. stability tradeoff identified but not fully resolved.

model_io.model: Electronic structure (DFT) and superconductivity assessment for carrier-doped hydrides

hypotheses.hypothesis: Average projected DOS is a superior descriptor of Tc than total or H-projected DOS alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; stronger correlation observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Carrier-doped hydrides can exhibit high-Tc superconductivity at ambient pressure
hypotheses.result: Supported by MgAlFeH₆ prediction (Tc ≈ 130 K)

title: Electron-phonon physics at the exascale: a hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP framework for scalable Wannier interpolation
task: GPU-accelerated Wannier interpolation for electron-phonon calculations at exascale
key_results: 29-fold speedup vs. MPI-only EPW v5.9; nearly ideal scaling to thousands of GPU nodes; enabled 20 nm topological stanene nanoribbon studies
comments: Exemplary HPC code modernization; demonstrated across multiple GPU architectures (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel) on leadership supercomputers.

model_io.model: EPW hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP framework for electron-phonon interpolation

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPU porting of Wannier interpolation achieves significant speedup and scalability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 29× speedup and exascale readiness demonstrated

title: Efficient foam-based thermal interface material functionalized with MWCNTs for CPU cooling applications: thermal performance modeling and Experimental studies
task: Develop PVF/MWCNT foam thermal interface material for CPU cooling
key_results: PVF/MWCNT at 4 wt% loading, 2 mm thickness, square geometry achieves minimum CPU temperature 66.72°C under 80 W load; thermal stability to 200°C
comments: Comprehensive thermal modeling and experimental validation; square geometry insight is practical, though mechanisms of thermal conductivity enhancement not deeply analyzed.

model_io.model: CPU thermal package model with PVF/MWCNT TIM interface

hypotheses.hypothesis: PVF/MWCNT composite with optimized geometry maximizes heat dissipation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 4 wt% MWCNT, square shape, 2 mm thickness optimal

hypotheses.hypothesis: Square geometry improves contact area and thermal performance vs. circular
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; square outperforms circular due to better CPU surface conformity

title: A low-complexity compact dual-polarized patch antenna with high isolation and low cross-polarization for in-band full-duplex applications
task: Design compact dual-polarized microstrip patch antenna for full-duplex wireless
key_results: Isolation >38 dB, cross-polarization <−40 dB, broadside gain 4.3 dBi, size 0.18λ × 0.18λ × 0.03λ at 5.6 GHz
comments: Capacitive loading elegantly addresses both size and polarization purity; performance metrics are state-of-the-art for compact designs.

model_io.model: Single-layer microstrip patch antenna with capacitive loading

hypotheses.hypothesis: Capacitive loading reduces size while suppressing cross-polarization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieved 0.18λ × 0.18λ × 0.03λ with −40 dB cross-polarization

title: Finite integration time can shift optimal sensitivity away from criticality
task: Determine optimal neural network tuning for finite integration time under sensitivity constraints
key_results: Optimal dynamic regime shifts away from criticality when integration time is finite; sensitivity depends on available observation window rather than theoretical maximum
comments: Important correction to criticality-sensitivity paradigm with practical implications for information processing systems.

model_io.model: Recurrent neural network with finite integration time

hypotheses.hypothesis: Maximal sensitivity occurs at criticality regardless of integration time
hypotheses.result: Refuted; optimal regime shifts away from criticality for finite times

title: Deep learning characterizes depression and suicidal ideation in young adults from eye movements
task: Classify depression and suicidal ideation from eye movement patterns during emotional reading tasks
key_results: AUC 0.793 for depression/suicidality vs. controls; AUC 0.826 for suicidality specifically; moderate differentiation between depressed and suicidal individuals (AUC 0.609)
comments: While eye tracking shows promise as an objective biomarker, the moderate performance in distinguishing depression from suicidality (AUC 0.609) suggests limitations in clinical utility for stratification.

model_io.model: Deep learning framework for eye movement classification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Eye movement patterns reflect attentional and mood biases associated with depression and suicidal ideation
hypotheses.result: Supported; discriminative patterns more pronounced during response generation and for negative sentiment stimuli

hypotheses.hypothesis: Oculomotor control is measurably impacted by emotional stimuli in individuals with depression/suicidality
hypotheses.result: Supported; eye tracking captures symptom severity differences, though specificity for suicidality vs. depression is limited
